verses.text: Darius orders a search, and the scroll of Cyrus's decree is found in the palace at Achmetha. The forgotten record comes to light at the needed hour. God preserves the evidence for His people and brings it forward in His time.

Darius orders a search, and the scroll of Cyrus's decree is found in the palace at Achmetha. The forgotten record comes to light at the needed hour. God preserves the evidence for His people and brings it forward in His time.

The decree commands the house rebuilt at royal expense and the temple vessels restored to their place. A pagan king's word secures God's house. The LORD had laid up provision for His people in a document written years before.

The decree commands the house rebuilt at royal expense and the temple vessels restored to their place. A pagan king's word secures God's house. The LORD had laid up provision for His people in a document written years before.

The decree commands the house rebuilt at royal expense and the temple vessels restored to their place. A pagan king's word secures God's house. The LORD had laid up provision for His people in a document written years before.

Darius charges Tatnai and his colleagues to leave the work alone and let the governor and elders build the house on its site. Opposition is overruled from above. The God who moved Cyrus now bends Darius to protect the work.

Darius charges Tatnai and his colleagues to leave the work alone and let the governor and elders build the house on its site. Opposition is overruled from above. The God who moved Cyrus now bends Darius to protect the work.

More than that, Darius orders their costs paid from the tribute and sacrifices supplied, that they may pray for the king and his sons. The enemy's appeal becomes the means of the work's funding. God turns intended harm into abundant help.

More than that, Darius orders their costs paid from the tribute and sacrifices supplied, that they may pray for the king and his sons. The enemy's appeal becomes the means of the work's funding. God turns intended harm into abundant help.

More than that, Darius orders their costs paid from the tribute and sacrifices supplied, that they may pray for the king and his sons. The enemy's appeal becomes the means of the work's funding. God turns intended harm into abundant help.

Darius decrees a fearful curse on any who alter his word or hinder the house, asking God to destroy such kings. The work is hedged with royal threat. The LORD can make even a heathen king its zealous guardian.

Darius decrees a fearful curse on any who alter his word or hinder the house, asking God to destroy such kings. The work is hedged with royal threat. The LORD can make even a heathen king its zealous guardian.

Tatnai obeys at once, and the elders prosper through the preaching of Haggai and Zechariah, finishing the house in the sixth year of Darius. Word and work together bring it to completion. God's house rises when His word is heard and obeyed.

Tatnai obeys at once, and the elders prosper through the preaching of Haggai and Zechariah, finishing the house in the sixth year of Darius. Word and work together bring it to completion. God's house rises when His word is heard and obeyed.

Tatnai obeys at once, and the elders prosper through the preaching of Haggai and Zechariah, finishing the house in the sixth year of Darius. Word and work together bring it to completion. God's house rises when His word is heard and obeyed.

They dedicate the house with joy and sacrifices and set the priests and Levites in their courses as written in the law of Moses. The finished house is given back to God in worship. Order and gladness mark the service He restores.

They dedicate the house with joy and sacrifices and set the priests and Levites in their courses as written in the law of Moses. The finished house is given back to God in worship. Order and gladness mark the service He restores.

They dedicate the house with joy and sacrifices and set the priests and Levites in their courses as written in the law of Moses. The finished house is given back to God in worship. Order and gladness mark the service He restores.

They keep the Passover with joy, for the LORD had made them glad and turned the king's heart toward them. The feast of redemption is renewed in the land. God who delivered them of old still turns kings' hearts and fills His people with joy.
